title: graphroot and runroot default to current directory
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)** /kind bug **Description** If storage.conf exists but does not contain graphroot or runroot, they will be saved in the database as "." in the first podman invocation, and further invocations will use the current directory for storage. **Steps to reproduce the issue:** 1. Delete all podman storage. 2. Create storage.conf with the following contents: ``` [storage] driver = "overlay" ``` 2. In an empty directory, run: ``` ls podman info ls podman info ls ``` **Describe the results you received:**
Output 
$ pwd
/tmp/a
$ ls
$ podman info
WARN[0000] Storage configuration is unset - using hardcoded default graph root "/var/lib/containers/storage"
WARN[0000] Storage configuration is unset - using hardcoded default graph root "/var/lib/containers/storage"
WARN[0000] Storage configuration is unset - using hardcoded default graph root "/var/lib/containers/storage"
Error: no storage root specified: missing necessary StoreOptions
$ ls
$ podman info
time="2021-12-01T11:32:33-05:00" level=warning msg="Storage configuration is unset - using hardcoded default graph root \"/var/lib/containers/storage\""
time="2021-12-01T11:32:33-05:00" level=warning msg="Storage configuration is unset - using hardcoded default graph root \"/var/lib/containers/storage\""
time="2021-12-01T11:32:33-05:00" level=warning msg="Storage configuration is unset - using hardcoded default graph root \"/var/lib/containers/storage\""

mounts  overlay  overlay-containers  overlay-images  overlay-layers  overlay-locks  storage.lock  tmp  userns.lock
**Describe the results you expected:** Podman does not use the current directory for storage. **Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):** **Output of `podman version`:** ``` Version: 3.4.2 API Version: 3.4.2 Go Version: go1.17.2 Built: Sun Nov 28 11:27:47 2021 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 ``` **Output of `podman info --debug`:** See above. **Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):** ``` [binary R ] app-emulation/podman-3.4.2-1::gentoo USE="-apparmor -btrfs -fuse -rootless (-selinux)" 0 KiB ``` **Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)** Yes **Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
